title: rudbeckia serotina var. corymbifera
intro: Rudbeckia serotina var. corymbifera, also known as the corymbiform coneflower, is a variety of perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Rudbeckia serotina var. corymbifera have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, with health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
